history: The client spent about a year working on a project in Europe, building a website and conducting interviews, and feels proud of this accomplishment with a desire to share the results. There is a history of procrastination and anxiety, which the client manages using strategies learned from a course and reminders. The client is currently facing ambiguity about life direction and choices, with many options to consider. The report suggests the client experiences tension between pride in achievements and anxiety about future decisions, and has a tendency to ruminate and feel overwhelmed when faced with uncertainty.
intermediate_belief: If I don't make the perfect choice, I'll regret it and others might judge me. I must be certain before committing to a direction. It's risky to act unless I'm sure I'm making the right decision.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't figure out my direction, maybe I'm just not good enough to succeed at anything.
coping_strategies: The client uses strategies learned from a course and reminders to manage daily life and reduce procrastination. When faced with ambiguity, the client tends to ruminate, delay decisions, and may seek reassurance or avoid committing to a path. These coping strategies provide temporary relief but maintain the cycle of anxiety and indecision.
situation: Facing ambiguity about life direction and choices, with many options to consider.
auto_thought: I have so many options and I don't know which one is right. If I choose wrong, I'll mess up my future.
behavior: I put off making a decision and spend a lot of time thinking about all the possibilities. I research options endlessly and sometimes avoid talking about my plans with others.
